_comment: Extracted findings for control RM-21D-01 from the deliberately non-conformant mock2 policy. mock2.docx itself is not committed, so this captures what the extraction layer LOCATES + QUOTES from it (design clauses on the supply-chain topic, several of which ARE the defects, and zero operating evidence). Used by tests/test_veto.py to regression-test the deterministic veto path: 13 design signals push design_done to 1.00 (and therefore L2/meets before the veto feature), while the 30-calendar-day supplier breach-notification window disqualifies the control.

facts.source_quote: Supplier Security Policy v1.0 governs third-party relationships
Each supplier is assigned a criticality rating at onboarding
Suppliers must notify us of a security breach within 30 calendar days
Supplier due diligence is satisfied by vendor self-attestation only
Fourth parties and sub-processors are excluded from this policy
Existing suppliers are grandfathered and not reassessed
Onboarding approval may be granted verbally by the sponsor
Standard contracts include a confidentiality clause
A right-to-audit clause may be requested where practical
Suppliers should follow good industry practice
A supplier register is maintained by procurement
Security requirements are referenced in the MSA template
The CISO owns the third-party risk procedure
